item.title: Getting Started
item.description: This guide gets a full MetaLoom stack running locally in a few minutes using the demo container. No build tools or configuration files are required.

item.title: Video Fingerprinting
item.description:
Intro

In this post I want to share my experiments on how to generate video fingerprints.

item.title: Day4 - Vert.x jOOQ PoC
item.description:
Intro

In this post I want to present a reactive database layer proof of concept implementation I did which will hopefully make its way into my MetaLoom project.

item.title: Day3 - Vert.x Dagger PoC
item.description:
Dagger / Vert.x

In this post I want to show a proof of concept project that makes use of Eclipse Vert.x 4.0.3 and Dagger 2.35.1 dependency injection. The PoC project contains a modularized Vert.x application that is using Maven modules and dependency injection. Vert.x is a project which provides libraries that can be used to create reactive applications on the JVM. The PoC contains a simple HTTP server.

item.title: Day2 - Project Setup
item.description:
Disclaimer

  • Coke vs. Sprite

  • Ketchup vs. Majo

  • Eclipse vs. IntelliJ

  • Java vs. Kotlin vs. Rust

item.title: Day1 - Project Design
item.description:
Basics

For Loom I knew what kind of building blocks were required since it is basically just a continuation of my work on Mesh.

item.title: Day0 - Let there be Loom
item.description:
What is Loom?

Loom is a headless media asset management server platform. It provides tools to inventory your media assets and extract the most information from them. The Loom Sever can be used to query the data in a structured way with either REST or GraphQL. Additional metadata can be stored in Loom alongside to your assets.

item.title: Administration Guide
item.description:
Docs

A

B

C

The docs

item.title: Artifacts
item.description:
Cortex publishes JVM modules and container deployment assets separately.

Maven Artifacts

See Maven Artifacts for the BOM-backed module list and node dependencies.

item.title: Artifacts
item.description:
Loom publishes JVM artifacts and container deployment assets separately.

Maven Artifacts

See Maven Artifacts for the BOM and the primary client and shared model modules.

item.title: Authentication
item.description: Loom uses JWT (JSON Web Token) bearer tokens for API authentication. OAuth2 integration is also available.

item.title: CLI Arguments & Env Settings
item.description: Cortex is configured entirely via CLI arguments and environment variables. Environment variables can be used as drop-in replacements for CLI flags, which is convenient for container-based deployments.

item.title: Configuration
item.description:
{
	"db": {
		"host": "localhost",
		"port": 5432
	},
	"server": {
		"port": 8080
	}
}

item.title: Configuration
item.description:
Loom is configured via a YAML file (loom.yml) with optional environment variable overrides.

Config File Locations

Loom looks for loom.yml in the following order (first match wins):

item.title: Containers
item.description:
Running with Docker

docker run --rm \
  -e LOOM_HOST=loom.internal \
  -e LOOM_PORT=7733 \
  -v /media/assets:/data \
  metaloom/cortex:latest \
  process run /data

item.title: Containers
item.description:
The Loom server is distributed as a Docker image.

Running with Docker

docker run -d \
  --name loom \
  -p 8092:8092 \
  -p 8091:8091 \
  -p 8989:8989 \
  -e LOOM_DB_HOST=postgres \
  -e LOOM_DB_PASSWORD=secret \
  -e LOOM_INITIAL_PASSWORD=admin123 \
  -v loom-config:/app/config \
  metaloom/loom:latest

item.title: Examples
item.description:
The examples/ directory in the source repository contains two Cortex example projects.

cortex-custom-node

A minimal Maven project that shows how to build a custom processing node.

item.title: Examples
item.description:
The /examples directory of the source repository contains runnable example projects.

Listing Examples

The examples/ Maven module contains sub-projects for both Loom and Cortex use cases.

item.title: Features
item.description:
Media Processing

Cortex can process any media file type — images, videos, audio, and documents. The specific analyses performed depend on which nodes are active in a pipeline.

item.title: Features
item.description: Loom provides a comprehensive set of features for managing digital media assets and their associated metadata.

item.title: Helm Chart
item.description: The Loom Helm chart is the Kubernetes deployment entry point for running the server in-cluster.

item.title: Java Client Usage
item.description:
The loom-rest-client module provides a typed HTTP client for JVM applications.

Maven Dependency

<dependency>
    <groupId>io.metaloom.loom.client</groupId>
    <artifactId>loom-rest-client</artifactId>
    <version>1.0.0-SNAPSHOT</version>
</dependency>

item.title: Johannes Schüth
item.description: Johannes main focus is backend development. But he does not mind devops and the occasional frontend development. He recharges his coding battery by outdoor photography or working on his IBM 5150. Besides retro computing his hobbies also include electronics and astronomy.

item.title: Loom & Cortex Interaction
item.description:
Loom and Cortex are complementary components that can be deployed together or used independently.

Architecture Overview

┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Loom (Media Asset Management Server)        │
│                                             │
│  REST API (:8092)  gRPC (:8091)             │
│  PostgreSQL database                        │
│  Pipeline definitions (stored in DB)        │
│  WebSocket event bus (/pipelines/events/ws) │
└───────────────────┬─────────────────────────┘
                    │  REST API (LoomClient)
                    │  Pipeline definitions
                    │  Asset results (hashes, thumbs, ...)
                    ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Cortex (Media Processing Engine)            │
│                                             │
│  Monitoring API (:8093)                     │
│  Pipeline executor (node graph)             │
│  Local meta-path cache                      │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────┘
        │
        │  reads media files
        ▼
   /media/assets/...

item.title: Maven Artifacts
item.description: All Cortex artifacts are published under the io.metaloom group and should generally be consumed with the shared BOM.

item.title: Maven Artifacts
item.description:
All Loom artifacts are published under the io.metaloom group.

BOM POM

Use the BOM to align versions across Loom and Cortex modules:

item.title: Monitoring API
item.description: Cortex exposes a lightweight monitoring/health endpoint that can be used by load balancers, Kubernetes liveness probes, and Prometheus scrapers.

item.title: Pipeline Mechanism
item.description: A pipeline is a directed acyclic graph (DAG) of processing nodes that Cortex executes on each media asset. Pipelines are defined in Loom (stored in the database) and loaded by Cortex at startup.

item.title: Pipeline Nodes
item.description: Cortex ships with a set of built-in processing nodes. Each node has a unique name that is used to reference it in CLI --actions flags and pipeline definitions.

item.title: REST API
item.description: Loom exposes its full functionality via a versioned REST API. All endpoints are rooted at /api/v1.

item.title: REST API
item.description:
Tip
Try the example
https://demo.metaloom.io/api/v1/demo/assets

API endpoints

Description API endpoint

Get the asset with the given UUID.

GET /api/v1/:projectName/assets/:assetUuid

 
{
	"uuid": "b5f26d5f-4ab0-4f99-a2f1-7be9f20e7a15",
	"filename": "sample.mp4"
}
